sections.paragraph:
m. (f. Nah 2:5)-

(1) riders, cavalry, Isa 21:7 and the beasts themselves; whence, verse Isa 21:9, רֶכֶב אִישׁ horses with (horse-) men.

(2) a chariot, i.q. מֶרְבָּבָה Jud. 5:28 pl. Son 1:9 but commonly collect. Gen 50:9. Especially military chariots, Exo 14:9, 17 Exo 14:17 15:19 1Ki 1:5 10:26 20:21 , and often. רֶכֶב בַּרְזֶל chariots with scythes, Jos 17:18. עָרֵי חָרֶכֶב towns where war-chariots were placed, 2Ch 1:14 8:6 9:25 .-Often רֶכֶב (like ἅρμα, in Hom.) refers mostly to the horses yoked to the chariots, and to the soldiers riding in the chariots, as 2Sa 8:4, “and David hamstrung all the chariots,” i.e. the horses of them; 2Sa 10:18, “and David slew seven hundred chariots of the Aramean s,” i.e. the horses and men of so many chariots; Eze 39:20 2Ki 7:14, שְׁנֵי רֶכֶב סוּסִים “two pairs of horses.”

(3) the upper millstone, Germ. der Laufer, Deu 24:6 2Sa 11:21.
